sections.heading: What this tracker does
sections.text:
The Portfolio Tracker is a single self-contained HTML file that runs entirely in your browser. This utilizes no server. There is no installation, and no account is required. It solves the core challenge of managing a complex digital asset portfolio across multiple wallets, liquidity pools, staking vaults, and speculative positions, all in one place with live prices.

Your data never leaves your machine. Everything is stored in your browser's local storage and a linked .json backup file on your own drive.

sections.heading: The seven tabs — what each does
sections.text:
📊 Summary — Portfolio command center. Total value, deployed capital, P&L across all areas, and the IL Break-Even Calculator for modeling new positions before committing capital.

🔁 LP Pools — Tracks concentrated liquidity positions (Uniswap v3, Aerodrome, Orca, Beefy vaults). Live pricing, impermanent loss calculation, yield logging, and the Beat HODL? scorecard.

🏦 Vaults — Single-asset yield positions: staking, lending, LSTs. Tracks APY, yield earned, and net result vs entry value.

💼 Holdings — Spot tokens across all wallets and chains. Cost basis, P&L, allocation %, and per-holding price alerts (buy trigger, stop loss, T1/T2/T3).

⚡ Hedge — Calculates optimal perpetual short positions on Hyperliquid to protect BTC, ETH, and SOL exposure. Live IV from Deribit, funding rates from Hyperliquid, and a 5-step formula produce a regime-based hedge ratio. Performance chart shows hedge effectiveness over time.

📁 History — Archives all closed pools, rebalances, yield logs, and transactions. Full editing capability on historical entries. Tax-ready CSV export.

💎 GEMs — Watchlist for speculative tokens. Buy triggers, stop losses, three take-profit tiers, Vol/MC momentum signals, and sparklines.

sections.heading: Key concepts to understand
sections.text:
Beat HODL? — The fundamental LP question: has the yield earned by providing liquidity outweighed the impermanent loss vs simply holding the tokens? Shown as ✓ YES / ✗ NO on every pool.

Hedge ratio — The percentage of your crypto exposure protected by a short position. Hard capped at 60% — you always retain meaningful upside exposure. The formula uses implied volatility, 7-day trend, and funding rate to recommend the optimal ratio automatically. Alerts are visible in the Header to suggest an update to a Hedge based on Market changes.

Net carry — Pool yield earned minus annual funding cost on the hedge. Positive carry means the hedge is net profitable.

Funded From Holdings — When adding capital to a pool or vault, this feature auto-deducts the source tokens from your Holdings and logs Transfer transactions — keeping all balances correct without manual editing.

Backup envelope — Your .json backup file contains everything: portfolio data, hedge chart history, activation token, Pushover keys, and settings. Linking the backup file on any machine restores the complete tracker instantly.

sections.heading: Getting started — four steps
sections.text:
1. Open in Chrome, Brave, or Edge — double-click the HTML file. Brave works identically to Chrome. Installation Instructions specific to your desired browser are available above on the Setup & Data Tab. Firefox is not recommended (File System API limitations).

2. Link your backup file — click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Change File → select or create a .json file in the same folder. Auto-save activates immediately (every 7 minutes, silently).

3. Add your first position — go to the tab that matches what you want to track. Click the + button. For LP Pools: enter both token symbols, current quantities, entry value, and your price range.

4. Refresh prices — click ↻ Refresh in the header. Prices update from CoinGecko and the live header bar populates. From here, everything is live.

sections.heading: Using Brave Browser
sections.text:
The tracker works identically in Brave as in Chrome — all features are supported. However, Brave's built-in Shields feature may block external data requests depending on your Shields setting.

If prices aren't loading, or live hedge/CPI data isn't updating:
Click the lion icon (🦁) in the address bar → set Shields to Standard (not Aggressive), or turn Shields off for this page.

Affected by Aggressive Shields: CoinGecko prices · Hyperliquid funding/IV · FRED CPI rate · Formspree feedback · imgbb screenshot uploads.

Not affected: All stored data, Holdings, Pools, Vaults, GEMs, History — these are local and always available regardless of Shields setting.

sections.heading: Data protection
sections.text:
Auto-save — every 7 minutes when a backup file is linked. Also saves when you switch away from the browser tab.

Manual save — ⛁ Data ▾ → Save / Export Backup downloads a dated .json backup to your drive.

After a cache clear — if your browser cache is cleared, localStorage is wiped. Re-open the tracker and link your .json backup file — all data restores immediately, including hedge chart history.

Moving to a new machine — copy the entire tracker folder (HTML file + backup .json + help_img/ folder). Open the HTML in Chrome and link the backup file. Activation, settings, and all data restore automatically.

sections.heading: Common first-time questions
sections.text:
Why isn't a token price loading? CoinGecko may be rate-limited — wait 60 seconds and click ↻ Refresh. If a specific token always shows — , its CoinGecko ID may be wrong — edit the entry and search for the correct ID.

How do I activate the tracker? Your Install ID is shown on the activation screen. Send it to the author and an activation code will be returned. The code is stored in your backup file and restores automatically on any future import.

What is the Hedge tab for? If you hold BTC, ETH, or SOL in pools or vaults, the Hedge tab helps you size a protective short position on Hyperliquid that offsets downside risk while keeping your upside exposure. It is entirely optional — all other tabs function independently.

Is my data safe? Yes — no data ever leaves your machine. The only external calls are to CoinGecko (prices), Deribit (implied volatility), Hyperliquid (funding rates), and a Cloudflare Worker (help content verification only). No portfolio data is transmitted in any of these calls.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Provides a bird's-eye view of your entire portfolio — total value, deployed capital, overall P&L, and a breakdown by area (Pools, Vaults, Holdings, GEMs).

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: All values are calculated in real time from live prices via CoinGecko. The IL Break-Even Calculator lets you model impermanent loss scenarios for any pool type before committing capital.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: No data entry here — this tab reads from all other tabs. Set your Inception Date by clicking the date field to track portfolio-wide performance from a specific start date.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Use the IL Calculator to model new positions before adding them. Click ↻ Refresh in the header to update all live prices.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text: Deployed = what you put in. Current = what it's worth now. Overall P&L = current minus deployed. Each area card shows its own deployed vs current vs P&L.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why does P&L show — ? Prices haven't loaded yet — click Refresh.
Why doesn't it match my pool totals exactly? Pool P&L includes yield; Holdings P&L is price appreciation only.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Tracks all active liquidity pool positions — token quantities, live values, yield harvested, range status, IL vs HODL comparison, and annualized returns.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Live prices fetch from CoinGecko. The ratio between Token A and Token B is compared to your set range (Low/High) to determine range status. IL is calculated using the pool model you select (v2, v3, Curve, Balancer).

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Pool. Enter both token symbols, current quantities, entry value, and your price range. Use ✎ to edit any row. Click ▶ on a row to log a yield harvest inline.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Log yield harvests regularly using the ▶ expand panel. When rebalancing use ✎ → Rebalance to reset your entry point. Use Close when exiting entirely.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Range badge: In Range · Watch (within 20% of boundary) · Act Now (within 10%) · OUT OF RANGE.
Net/Ann%: Total return including yield, annualized.
Beat HODL? Pool+Yield vs simply holding the tokens.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is Live Ratio showing — ? A token price failed to load — try Refresh.
What pool model should I use? Uniswap v3/Orca = v3, Uniswap v2/Sushi = v2, Curve/Beefy stable pairs = Curve, Balancer = Balancer.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Tracks single-asset yield positions — staking, lending vaults, LSTs, and any strategy where you deposit one token and earn yield on it over time.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Current value = Qty × Live Price. If you enter an APY, it estimates yield earned based on days since entry. You can override this with actual yield logged.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Vault. Enter the token, platform, quantity, APY (optional), entry date, and entry value. Update Qty when your staked amount changes. Enter actual Yield Earned $ when you harvest.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Update yield earned periodically. If you add more capital, edit the vault and adjust qty and entry value. Use Close via the Edit modal to archive to History when exiting.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Ann. Return: Combines price appreciation + yield, annualized. EST means it's using your APY estimate rather than logged yield.
Yield APR = pure yield return excluding price movement.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is Current showing — ? Token price not loaded — check the CoinGecko ID is correct.
My vault shows a loss — why? The token price may have fallen since entry. Entry value reflects what you deposited, not what it's worth now.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Tracks all spot holdings — tokens held in wallets, exchanges, or hardware devices. Shows live price, 24h change, average cost basis, current value, allocation %, and unrealized P&L.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Each row is one token/wallet/chain combination. Average cost updates automatically using weighted average when you buy more. The Group button clusters holdings by wallet for a cleaner view.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Holding or use Buy/Sell/Swap/Transfer actions on an existing row. For buys, enter qty and price — cost basis is calculated automatically. For stablecoins, price locks at $1.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Use the 🔔 alert button on any row to set price alerts (buy trigger, stop loss, TP1/2/3). Use Swap to record token-to-token trades. Use Transfer to move between wallets while preserving cost basis.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Avg Cost: Your blended entry price across all purchases.
Alloc %: This holding as a percentage of total spot portfolio value.
P&L $/%: Unrealized gain/loss vs your cost basis.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why does a token show — for price? The CoinGecko ID may be wrong — edit and search for the correct token.
How do I record a sale? Use the Sell action on the holding row — it reduces qty and logs the transaction.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Calculates optimal short hedge sizes for BTC, ETH, and SOL to protect your LP and vault positions from downside price risk. Tracks open shorts on Hyperliquid and monitors funding rates.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: The optimal ratio is calculated from IV (implied volatility), 7-day trend, funding rate, your pool yield APY, and BTC/asset stability signals. Funding rates and IV fetch live from Hyperliquid and Deribit.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Use the slider on each asset card to set your hedge ratio. Click + Open Position to log a short — enter asset, margin ($), leverage, and entry price. Log HL deposits/withdrawals via the HL Deposit/Withdraw button.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Click ↻ Refresh to fetch latest funding rates and IV. Close positions via the Close button on Open Positions. Check the calculation detail (▶ Show) on each card to understand how the ratio was derived.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Funding Rate: Negative = shorts paying longs (costs you). Positive = longs paying shorts (you earn).
Rate / Funding column: Current 8hr rate · cumulative net earned or paid.
Net carry: Annual pool yield minus annual funding cost — positive means hedging is net profitable.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is funding rate showing — ? Click Refresh — Hyperliquid data fetches on demand.
What does negative net carry mean? Funding cost exceeds pool yield — consider reducing hedge ratio.
What is the Limit Buy checkbox? Signals recovery conviction and reduces the recommended hedge by ~18%.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Archives all closed and rebalanced pool positions, yield harvest logs, and transactions. Your permanent record for performance analysis and tax reporting.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Positions are auto-archived when you Close or Rebalance from the LP Pools tab. Yield entries are linked to their pool. The Loss Status flag lets you mark positions where funds were lost to hacks, mismanagement, or theft.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Use ⚙ on any history row to edit dates, name, type, and Loss Status. To mark a loss: click ⚙, set Loss Status (Unrecoverable / Partial / Legal Action), and add a note describing what happened.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Add yield entries to closed pools via ➕ Add Yield Entry. Export to CSV for tax reporting. Update Loss Status if funds are partially recovered or legal proceedings conclude.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Net Result: Exit value + yield - entry value.
Beat HODL? Did pool + yield outperform simply holding the tokens?
Loss badges: ⚠ Unrecoverable · 🔶 Partial · ⚖️ Legal Action · ✅ Recovered.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
How do I record a hack or theft? Close the vault/pool at $0 exit value, then edit the history entry and set Loss Status to Unrecoverable with a note.
Can I change the loss status later? Yes — fully editable at any time via the ⚙ button.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Watchlist for speculative tokens — tracks live price, 24h change, Vol/MC momentum, entry price, targets (T1/T2/T3), stop loss, and buy triggers. Sends Pushover alerts when prices hit your levels.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Prices fetch from CoinGecko. Vol/MC (Volume/Market Cap ratio) is tracked over 1hr, 24hr, and 48hr windows to detect momentum shifts before price moves. Sparklines show 7-day price history at a glance.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add GEM and search for the token. Set your entry price, qty held, and targets. Use the ▶ expand panel on any row to set Stop Loss, T1/T2/T3, and Add trigger inline.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Set up Pushover in Settings (⚙ icon) for phone alerts. Use ✔ Executed? to confirm TP sells and auto-update your qty. The thesis editor (📝) lets you record your investment case for each token.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Vol/MC columns (1h/24h/48h): % change in volume-to-market-cap ratio vs that window. Rising Vol/MC often precedes price moves.
Alert % badge: Custom mover alert threshold — fires when 24h move exceeds this %.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is live price showing — ? The CoinGecko ID is wrong — edit and search again.
What's the difference between Alert In and T1? Alert In fires when price drops TO your level (buy). T1/T2/T3 fire when price rises TO your target (sell).
How do I get phone alerts? Install Pushover app, create account, enter User Key and App Token in Settings.

sections.heading: Windows — Step 1: Create your working folder
sections.text:
Open File Explorer (press Win + E). Navigate to Documents. Right-click in empty space → New → Folder → name it PortfolioTracker.

Move Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html into this folder. This is your permanent home for the tracker — never move it after setup.

sections.heading: Windows — Step 2: Tell Chrome where to save files
sections.text:
In Chrome, paste this into the address bar and press Enter:

chrome://settings/downloads

Turn ON the toggle: "Ask where to save each file before downloading"

Edge users: use edge://settings/downloads instead.

This ensures your data file saves to your working folder rather than disappearing into Downloads.

sections.heading: Windows — Step 3: Link your data file
sections.text:
Open the tracker from your PortfolioTracker folder (not from Downloads or a browser bookmark).

Click ▶ Start Free Trial on the activation screen.

In the header click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File.

Navigate to your PortfolioTracker folder. Type portfolio_data.json as the filename → click Save.

The status dot in the header turns green ● — auto-save is now active every 7 minutes.

sections.heading: Windows — Step 4: Create a desktop shortcut
sections.text:
Open your PortfolioTracker folder in File Explorer. Right-click Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html → Send to → Desktop (create shortcut).

Always open the tracker via this shortcut. Never open it from Downloads or a browser bookmark — the file link won't work correctly from there.

sections.heading: Mac — Step 1: Create your working folder
sections.text:
Open Finder (the smiley face icon in your Dock). Click Documents in the left sidebar.

Press Cmd + Shift + N to create a new folder → name it PortfolioTracker.

Move Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html into this folder.

sections.heading: Mac — Step 2: Tell Chrome where to save files
sections.text:
In Chrome, paste this into the address bar and press Enter:

chrome://settings/downloads

Turn ON the toggle: "Ask where to save each file before downloading"

Mac extra step: The first time the tracker auto-saves, Chrome shows a bar saying "Allow this site to download multiple files?" — click Allow. This only appears once.

If you missed it: Chrome menu (⋮) → Settings → Privacy and security → Site settings → Additional permissions → Automatic downloads → add the tracker to the Allowed list.

sections.heading: Mac — Step 3: Link your data file
sections.text:
Open the tracker from your PortfolioTracker folder in Finder (not from Downloads).

Click ▶ Start Free Trial. In the header click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File.

Navigate to your PortfolioTracker folder. Type portfolio_data.json → click Save.

The dot turns green ●.

sections.heading: Mac — If Chrome loses access after restart
sections.text:
Mac Chrome resets file write permissions when you restart the browser — this is a Mac security feature, not a bug.

You will see an amber banner at the bottom of the screen: "Auto-save permission lost".

Click 🔓 Restore Access → Chrome asks you to confirm — click Allow. Auto-save resumes immediately.

If the banner does not appear, click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File and relink your file.

sections.heading: Mac — Step 4: Create a desktop alias
sections.text:
Open your PortfolioTracker folder in Finder. Hold Cmd + Opt and drag Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html to your Desktop.

This creates an alias shortcut. Always open the tracker from here — not from Downloads or Safari.

sections.heading: Brave (Windows) — Step 1: Create your working folder
sections.text:
Open File Explorer (press Win + E). Click Documents in the left sidebar.

Right-click in empty space → New → Folder → name it PortfolioTracker.

Move Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html into this folder. This is your permanent home — never move it after setup.

sections.heading: Brave (Windows) — Step 2: Tell Brave where to save files
sections.text:
In Brave, paste this into the address bar and press Enter:

brave://settings/downloads

Turn ON the toggle: "Ask where to save each file before downloading"

This ensures your data file saves to your working folder instead of disappearing into Downloads.

Also check Brave Shields: Click the lion icon 🦁 in the address bar while the tracker is open → make sure Shields is set to Standard (not Aggressive). Aggressive Shields blocks live price updates.

sections.heading: Brave (Windows) — Step 3: Link your data file
sections.text:
Open the tracker from your PortfolioTracker folder (not from Downloads or a bookmark).

Click ▶ Explore the Demo on the welcome screen.

In the header click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File.

A Save dialog opens — navigate to your PortfolioTracker folder. Type portfolio_data.json as the filename → click Save.

The status dot in the header turns green ● — auto-save is now active. If Brave asks "Allow this site to save files?" click Allow.

sections.heading: Brave (Windows) — Step 4: Create a desktop shortcut
sections.text:
Open your PortfolioTracker folder in File Explorer. Right-click Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html → Send to → Desktop (create shortcut).

Always open the tracker from this desktop shortcut. Never open it from Downloads or a Brave bookmark — the file link will not reconnect correctly from there.

sections.heading: Brave (Mac) — Step 1: Create your working folder
sections.text:
Open Finder (the smiley face in your Dock). Click Documents in the left sidebar.

Press Cmd + Shift + N to create a new folder → name it PortfolioTracker.

Move Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html into this folder.

sections.heading: Brave (Mac) — Step 2: Tell Brave where to save files
sections.text:
In Brave, paste this into the address bar and press Enter:

brave://settings/downloads

Turn ON the toggle: "Ask where to save each file before downloading"

Mac extra step: The first time the tracker auto-saves, Brave may show a bar at the top saying "Allow this site to download multiple files?" — click Allow. This only appears once.

If you missed it: click the lion icon 🦁 in the address bar → Site settings → scroll to Automatic downloads → set to Allow.

Also check Shields: Click the lion icon 🦁 → set Shields to Standard so prices and live data load correctly.

sections.heading: Brave (Mac) — Step 3: Link your data file
sections.text:
Open the tracker from your PortfolioTracker folder in Finder (not from Downloads).

Click ▶ Explore the Demo. In the header click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File.

Navigate to your PortfolioTracker folder. Type portfolio_data.json → click Save.

The dot turns green ●. If the dot stays grey, close and reopen the tracker from the same folder.

sections.heading: Brave (Mac) — If Brave loses access after restart
sections.text:
Like Chrome on Mac, Brave may reset file write permissions when you restart the browser — this is a Mac security feature.

You will see an amber banner: "Auto-save permission lost".

Click 🔓 Restore Access → Brave asks you to confirm → click Allow. Auto-save resumes immediately.

If the banner does not appear: click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File and relink your portfolio_data.json file.

sections.heading: Brave (Mac) — Step 4: Create a desktop alias
sections.text:
Open your PortfolioTracker folder in Finder. Hold Cmd + Opt and drag Portfolio_Tracker_Demo.html to your Desktop.

This creates an alias shortcut. Always open the tracker from here — not from Downloads.

sections.heading: Fix: Data file is saving to the wrong folder
sections.text:
1. Click ⛁ Data ▾ → Save / Export Backup — save a copy somewhere safe first.
2. Click ⛁ Data ▾ → ⚠ Reset Data Link to clear the wrong link.
3. Click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File → navigate to your PortfolioTracker folder → save as portfolio_data.json.
4. The dot turns green ● — done.

sections.heading: Fix: Numbered copies appearing (autosave (1).json, (2).json…)
sections.text:
This happens when Chrome does not have "Ask where to save" turned on, so copies pile up in Downloads instead of overwriting the linked file.

1. Find the highest-numbered copy — that is your most recent data.
2. Click ⛁ Data ▾ → Import Backup → select that file to restore your data.
3. Delete all the numbered copies from your Downloads folder.
4. Enable "Ask where to save": paste chrome://settings/downloads in Chrome → turn on the toggle.
5. Click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File → save to your PortfolioTracker folder.

sections.heading: Fix: Status dot is grey or orange — data not saving
sections.text:
Grey dot means no file is linked. Click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File → navigate to your working folder → select your existing portfolio_data.json.

Orange dot or the amber "Auto-save permission lost" banner means the file is linked but Chrome lost write access. Click 🔓 Restore Access in the banner, or use ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File to relink.

sections.heading: Fix: I cleared my browser cache and lost my data
sections.text:
If you had a linked file, your data is safe — the portfolio_data.json file on your computer is the permanent copy. Browser cache clearing only removes the browser's temporary copy.

Click ⛁ Data ▾ → Import Backup → select your portfolio_data.json file → then relink it via ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Create File.

sections.heading: v47.32  — CURRENT
sections.text:
Version bump to ensure all demo users receive the full set of recent updates.

✅ All recent updates now delivered
This version ensures all demo users see the complete set of recent improvements: pool close wallet dropdown with Token B override, per-token transaction logging on close and rebalance, independent Hedge ratio alert pill, Brave browser setup guide, qty auto-mirror on new pool entry, CPI benchmark badge, screenshot paste in feedback, and hedge ratio alert banner on Summary page.

sections.heading: v47.31
sections.text:
Pool close redesigned with wallet dropdown, per-token transaction logging, rebalance exit logging, Brave browser support, and qty auto-mirror on new pool entry.

📤 Pool Close — wallet dropdown with Token B override
The Close/Rebalance modal now uses a wallet dropdown pre-filled with the pool default DeFi wallet — same as the entry side. A "Token B returns to different wallet?" toggle adds a second wallet selector for the edge case where tokens go to different wallets on exit.

📋 Per-token transaction logging on close and rebalance
Each token now gets its own transaction log entry when a pool is closed or rebalanced — showing the exact qty, price at exit, and destination wallet. Previously a single combined entry was logged. Rebalances now also log the exit side before resetting for the new leg.

🦁 Brave browser — full setup guide and compatibility note
The Help → Setup & Data Guide now includes step-by-step Brave instructions for both Windows and Mac — matching the Chrome guide exactly. A new Brave Browser section in the Overview tab explains Shields settings and which features require Standard mode.

🔢 New pool entry — qty auto-mirrors between Entry and Current fields
When adding a new pool, typing a quantity in either the Qty at Entry or Current Qty field now automatically fills the other if it is blank. At the moment of first entry they are always equal, so there is no longer any need to type the same number twice. Auto-mirror is disabled when editing an existing pool.

sections.heading: v47.30
sections.text:
Hedge ratio alert — Summary page and phone alerts now notify when the suggested hedge ratio shifts significantly from your current position.

⚖ Hedge Ratio Alert — know when to rebalance your hedge
When the model suggested hedge ratio for BTC, ETH, or SOL shifts more than 10 percentage points from your current ratio, a banner appears on the Summary page showing the current vs suggested ratio and the reason (IV change, trend shift, funding rate). A Pushover phone alert fires simultaneously. Alerts are throttled to once per 4 hours per asset to avoid noise.

sections.heading: v47.29
sections.text:
CPI benchmark badge — pool rows now show whether annualized net return beats inflation, fetched live from FRED.

📊 CPI Benchmark Badge — beat inflation at a glance
Each pool row now shows a small ▲CPI (green) or ▼CPI (red) badge next to the annualized return, comparing your net return against the current CPI inflation rate. The rate is fetched automatically from the Federal Reserve FRED database on startup and cached for 24 hours. You can also set a manual rate or refresh it live in Settings → CPI Benchmark Badge. Only shown for pools open 7+ days.

sections.heading: v47.28
sections.text:
Feedback modal upgraded — paste a screenshot directly into the form and it uploads automatically with your message.

📸 Feedback — paste screenshot directly in the modal
The Feedback modal now includes a paste zone for screenshots. Take a screenshot (Cmd+Shift+4 on Mac, Win+Shift+S on Windows), click the dashed box, and press Cmd+V / Ctrl+V. A thumbnail preview appears instantly. When you send feedback the screenshot uploads automatically to imgbb and the link is included in the email — no extra steps needed.

sections.heading: v47.27
sections.text:
Demo feedback channel — send questions, bug reports or suggestions directly to the developer via GitHub.

📬 Feedback button — send questions or reports from within the app
A new Feedback button in the header opens a short form where you can choose a type (Question, Bug Report, Suggestion), write your message, and optionally add your name. Clicking Open GitHub pre-fills a GitHub issue with your message and the current version number — just hit Submit. A screenshot tip is shown in the modal explaining how to capture and attach a screenshot directly in GitHub before submitting.

sections.heading: v47.26
sections.text:
Funded From Holdings redesigned — cleaner layout, default wallet pre-filled, optional per-token wallet override.

📤 Funded From Holdings — redesigned for clarity
The Funded From Holdings section in Add Capital now shows two simple qty fields (one per token) followed by a single wallet dropdown pre-filled with the pool\'s default DeFi wallet — consistent with how other modals handle wallet selection. For the edge case where Token B comes from a different source, a "Token B from different wallet?" toggle reveals a second wallet selector. The old freeform three-column grid that clipped off the right edge of the modal has been removed entirely.

sections.heading: v47.25
sections.text:
Add Capital redesigned for natural token-first input, yield log now groups multi-token harvests into single rows.

➕ Add Capital — enter token quantities, not dollars
The LP Add Capital modal now asks for the qty of each token you added (e.g. 0.5 AAVE + 0.02 WETH) — matching exactly what the platform shows you. The dollar value is calculated automatically from live prices and shown as "Calculated $ Added" before you confirm, so you can verify the number looks right without doing any arithmetic yourself. The new total entry $ is also shown as a read-only confirmation.

📋 Yield log — multi-token harvests shown as one row
When two tokens are harvested from the same pool on the same date (e.g. AAVE + WETH from an AAVE/WETH pool), they now appear as a single combined row in the History yield log rather than two separate rows. Token symbol and quantity are shown side by side, with a combined USD total. Individual edit and delete buttons are still available for each token entry. This makes the log much easier to scan, especially when searching back through many months of harvests.

🛡️ Data integrity — embedded backup always current
The HTML file now updates its own embedded data snapshot on every save via the new Link App File feature (💾 Data ▾ → Auto-Update App File). Previously the snapshot was frozen at the time the file was built, meaning a reboot with cleared browser storage would silently restore stale data — resurrecting closed pools, old vault balances, and positions that had been rebalanced. Link the HTML file once and it stays permanently in sync.

🔄 Startup reliability — backup file always loads on open
On startup the app now always compares your linked backup file against browser storage and loads whichever is newer. Previously a hidden file:// protocol restriction caused the backup file to be completely ignored on startup when running as a local file, meaning browser storage (which clears on reboot) was the only source of truth. The backup file is now always authoritative.

⬆ Built-in update check — works from any local file
A new Update button in the header and Check for Update entry in the Data menu open the download page directly. These work from file:// without any network fetch, so all users can check for new versions regardless of how the app is opened. The link is also visible on the activation screen before login.

🔒 Pool and vault actions locked to the correct record
Add Capital, Close/Rebalance, A↔B Flip, and Save Pool now carry the ID of the specific pool or vault you opened — locked from the moment you clicked edit. Previously a single shared variable held the active ID, which could be overwritten if you updated another pool before completing your action, causing data to be written to the wrong pool. The same fix is applied to the vault modal.

➕ Add Capital — vault mode simplified
For Beefy-style vault pools, Add Capital previously asked for the same dollar figure in two different fields. It now asks only for: the token qty you deposited (e.g. 0.5 ETH), the current pool value shown on the platform after your deposit, and which wallet the funds came from. The dollar amount added is calculated automatically — no duplicate entry.

🐛 Wrong pool receiving capital addition — fixed
After updating several pools current values and then opening Add Capital on the Aerodrome msETH/WETH pool, the capital addition was incorrectly applied to the Beefy Ethereum msETH/WETH pool instead. This was caused by the stale global pool ID. With this fix the pool ID is passed explicitly at every step — it cannot drift.

sections.heading: v47.21
sections.text:
Unified hardened storage (cpfVault): all critical data now lives in a single IndexedDB store that survives any cache clear. Ghost reappearance of closed pools/vaults fixed.

🔒 Unified vault storage — immune to cache clears
All critical settings — Pushover keys, PIN, activation code, default wallet — now live in a single dedicated IndexedDB store (cpfVault). This store is completely separate from localStorage and survives all forms of browser cache clearing, including "Clear all site data". On boot, the vault is loaded first before any auth or PIN check runs, so nothing is ever lost after a cache wipe or version update.

👻 Ghost closed pools/vaults reappearing — fixed
Closed LPs and vaults were reappearing after loading a new version of the app. The embedded backup data baked into each HTML file was being merged into your live data on every single boot, resurrect­ing positions you had already closed. The merge now only runs on a genuine fresh install (empty storage), never on normal sessions or version updates.

🔑 PIN now fully hardened
The PIN hash was previously stored only in localStorage — the one place that gets wiped. It is now stored in cpfVault as the primary location, with localStorage as a fast-read cache. After any cache clear, the PIN is restored from the vault before the lock screen appears, so the app never loses your PIN.

🧹 Storage architecture consolidated
The four previously separate storage locations (cpf_act_db, cpf_handles_db kv store, cpf_pushKey localStorage, cpf_pin_hash localStorage) are now unified through the single cpfVault path. All writes go through vaultSet(), which writes to IDB and keeps a localStorage mirror automatically. No more scattered, inconsistent write paths.

sections.heading: v47.15
sections.text:
Beefy vault HODL baseline now calculates correctly using LP token price — both pools now correctly show Beat HODL ✓ Yes.

🏦 Beefy vault HODL baseline fixed — LP token price formula
The HODL baseline for like-pair Beefy vaults (cbETH/ETH, msETH/WETH) was previously calculated using the raw ETH price, which is wrong — these vaults issue LP tokens whose price moves independently of ETH. The correct formula is: original LP tokens deposited × current LP token price (= current value ÷ current LP token count). This now matches Beefy\'s own Position Performance figures exactly, and both pools correctly show Beat HODL ✓ Yes.

🔧 Self-heal logic removed
A "self-heal" block was automatically overwriting entryPriceA on every load, replacing the correct ETH price at deposit with a calculated LP token price. This corrupted the HODL formula and fought against correct manual data entry. It has been removed — entryPriceA is set once at deposit and never touched by the app again.

⏱️ HODL baseline no longer flashes wrong value on startup
The app previously seeded prices from the saved backup before CoinGecko responded, causing the HODL baseline to briefly show a wrong value. Prices now start empty and the HODL baseline shows — until the first successful price fetch, then displays the correct figure.

sections.heading: v47.07
sections.text:
Vault-mode pools (Beefy etc) no longer show a misleading IL / Beat HODL calculation.

🏦 Vault-mode pools: IL and Beat HODL now show N/A
Beefy and other vault-mode pools had no LP impermanent loss — they hold a single token with auto-compounding yield, not a two-token ratio position. Previously the IL and Beat HODL columns were calculated using stale or inapplicable entry prices, producing misleading results like -80% IL. They now correctly show — (N/A) since the HODL comparison does not apply to single-asset vaults.

sections.heading: v47.06
sections.text:
Vault Edit modal now shows Current Value $ instead of Yield Earned — just enter what the platform shows.

🏦 Vault edit: enter Current Value instead of Yield Earned
The Edit Vault modal now shows a Current Value $ field instead of Yield Earned $. Enter the total value shown on your platform (e.g. $10,250) and the app back-calculates yield automatically as Current Value minus Entry Value. A live price estimate is shown as a reference. The field is only shown when editing an existing vault — not when adding a new one.

sections.heading: v47.05
sections.text:
Single-instance enforcement and PIN display race condition fixed.

🪟 Only one instance can run at a time
Opening the tracker in a second tab now detects the existing instance via BroadcastChannel. The duplicate tab shows an "Already open" message with a Close button, while the original tab is focused. Works on file:// and http:// in Chrome and Edge.

🔒 PIN screen no longer disappears on startup
A race condition caused the PIN lock screen to flash away before the user entered their PIN. The activation screen was restoring app visibility before initPIN() had a chance to show the lock. Fixed by moving visibility restoration exclusively into hideLock() and initPIN(), so the app content is never revealed until the correct PIN is entered.

sections.heading: v47.04
sections.text:
Rebalance now clears all old token quantities and opens the Edit modal so you enter fresh numbers for the new leg.

⇄ Rebalance clears all entry fields and prompts for new quantities
After archiving the old leg, all quantity, value, and range fields are now cleared to zero/blank. The Edit modal reopens immediately so you can enter the new Token A and B quantities for the new position. Entry Qty and Current Qty start equal (as they should at pool entry) and diverge naturally as the pool runs. Entry prices are set to current live prices at the moment of rebalance.

sections.heading: v47.03
sections.text:
IL Calculator: Holding Period and Monthly Yield fields are now side by side, bringing the Calculate button into view without scrolling.

⚖️ Calculate button always visible on Summary tab
The Holding Period and Monthly Yield $ fields in the IL Break-Even Calculator are now on a single row side by side. This saves enough vertical space to bring the Calculate → button into view on a standard screen without scrolling — all fields and labels are still present.

sections.heading: v47.02
sections.text:
Data menu now scrolls so Export Backup button is always visible; file:// notice expanded with Python server auto-save setup guide.

💾 Data menu always fully visible
The Data ▾ dropdown now has a max-height with internal scrolling, so the Export Backup Now button and file:// notice are never pushed off-screen regardless of window height.

🖥️ Auto-save setup guide built into the app
The file:// warning section now includes a step-by-step guide to enable full auto-save: run "python -m http.server 8080" from your tracker folder, then open http://localhost:8080/ in Chrome. Both the command and URL have one-click copy buttons. This unlocks auto-save every 7 minutes, save-on-tab-switch, and the ⛁ Link / Create File feature.

sections.heading: v47.01
sections.text:
IDB persistence fix: hedge chart history, Pushover keys, and DeFi wallet now survive browser cache clears.

🛡️ Hedge chart history survives cache clears
The hedge performance chart history (HPC) is now mirrored to IndexedDB in addition to localStorage. IndexedDB is not cleared when you wipe browser cache — so your chart history will be restored automatically the next time you open the tracker, even after a full cache clear.

🔔 Pushover keys survive cache clears
Pushover User Key and App Token are now stored in IndexedDB as well as localStorage. After a cache clear, both keys are automatically restored into DB.settings within 1 second of startup — no need to re-enter them.

👛 DeFi wallet setting survives cache clears
The DeFi Wallet selection (Settings → Wallets) is now mirrored to IndexedDB. It will be restored automatically on next startup if localStorage was cleared.

💾 Backup import now also writes to IDB
When you import a backup .json file, the hedge chart history and Pushover keys inside it are now also written to IndexedDB immediately — so they persist through any subsequent cache clear without needing another import.

sections.heading: v47.00
sections.text:
Push notification overhaul: separate cooldowns per alert type, per-pool alert tiers, GEM notification fixes, and hedge modal auto-fill.

🔔 Separate cooldowns per alert type
Pool Out of Range, Act Now (≤5%), Watch (≤15%), and GEM/Holdings alerts now each have their own cooldown setting (Settings → 📱 Phone Alerts). Defaults: Out 30 min, Act Now 20 min, Watch 60 min, GEM 20 min. Previously all shared one timer — this caused Watch alerts to repeat as often as urgent alerts.

🏊 Per-pool alert tier: Silent / Standard / Sensitive
Each pool now has an Alert Tier setting (in the pool edit modal, and during Add Pool setup). Silent = no Pushover at all (ideal for Beefy/auto-managed vaults). Standard = global cooldowns. Sensitive = cooldowns halved (use for volatile pairs like ETH/USDC near range edge).

💎 GEM 24h mover alerts fixed
A bug caused GEM up/down mover alerts to never re-fire after their first send — the condition-clear reset was calling .delete() on a plain object instead of the correct delete operator. Fixed. GEM alerts now correctly re-fire after their cooldown elapses.

⚡ Hedge alert engine fixed
The hedge TP/SL/liquidation alert section was using Set-style .has()/.add()/.delete() API on a plain object — all silently broken. Converted to the shared _canAlert/_markAlerted/_alertReset system with proper cooldown support.

💰 Hedge modal: Margin $ ↔ Hedge $ auto-fill
The Add Hedge Position modal now shows both Margin $ (your collateral) and Hedge Size $ (notional = margin × leverage) with live auto-fill between them. Enter either field and the other updates instantly. Short qty also auto-calculates from Hedge $ ÷ Entry price. Example: Margin $1,000 at 3× → Hedge $3,000 auto-filled.

📈 Hedge chart now builds history across sessions
The Hedge Performance Chart accumulates price history between sessions using IV-adaptive sampling (1–10 min intervals based on current volatility, plus a 0.5% spot-move trigger). The 1H / 6H / 1D / 7D / All time windows are now meaningful as history grows over days and weeks.

📖 Live help documentation site
A full help site is now live at jdgoldstein.github.io/portfoliotracker-help — searchable, dark mode, tab-by-tab documentation, FAQ, and changelog. The tracker syncs help content in the background on startup. Edit help locally via ✏ Edit, then click ⬆ Export to GitHub to publish updates.

⛁ Versioned backup envelope (v2)
Backup files now use a structured envelope: { db, hpcHistory, schemaVersion: 2 }. Hedge chart history travels with your backup and restores automatically on import. Feed data (CoinGecko prices, hedgeCache) is no longer written to backup files — keeps files clean and small.

⏱ Auto-save every 7 minutes, silently
Auto-save interval reduced from 10 to 7 minutes. No more toast notification on every auto-save tick — the timestamp is visible in the ⛁ menu.

notes: Version bump to ensure all demo users receive the full set of recent updates.
✅ All recent updates now delivered: This version ensures all demo users see the complete set of recent improvements.
Pool close redesigned with wallet dropdown, per-token transaction logging, rebalance exit logging, Brave browser support, and qty auto-mirror on new pool entry.

📤 Pool Close — wallet dropdown with Token B override: The Close/Rebalance modal now uses a wallet dropdown pre-filled with the pool default DeFi wallet. A "Token B returns to different wallet?" toggle adds a second wallet selector.

📋 Per-token transaction logging on close and rebalance: Each token now gets its own transaction log entry when a pool is closed or rebalanced — showing the exact qty, price at exit, and destination wallet.
🦁 Brave browser — full setup guide: The Help → Setup & Data Guide now includes step-by-step Brave instructions for both Windows and Mac.
🔢 New pool entry — qty auto-mirrors between Entry and Current fields: When adding a new pool, typing a quantity in either field auto-fills the other if blank.
Hedge ratio alert — Summary page and phone alerts notify when the suggested hedge ratio shifts significantly.
⚖ Hedge Ratio Alert — know when to rebalance your hedge: When the suggested hedge ratio shifts more than 10 percentage points a banner appears on Summary and a Pushover alert fires.
CPI benchmark badge — pool rows now show whether annualized net return beats inflation.
📊 CPI Benchmark Badge — beat inflation at a glance: Each pool row now shows a ▲CPI (green) or ▼CPI (red) badge comparing your net return against the current CPI inflation rate fetched from FRED.
Feedback modal upgraded — paste a screenshot directly into the form.
📸 Feedback — paste screenshot directly in the modal: The Feedback modal now includes a paste zone — screenshot uploads automatically to imgbb with your message.
Demo feedback channel — send questions or bug reports directly to the developer.
📬 Feedback button: A new Feedback button in the header opens a short form for questions, bug reports, or suggestions.
Funded From Holdings redesigned — cleaner layout, default wallet pre-filled.
📤 Funded From Holdings — redesigned for clarity: Two qty fields, single wallet dropdown pre-filled with pool default DeFi wallet, optional Token B wallet override.
Add Capital redesigned for token-first input, yield log groups multi-token harvests.
➕ Add Capital — enter token quantities, not dollars: Enter qty of each token — dollar value calculated automatically from live prices.
📋 Yield log — multi-token harvests shown as one row: Same-pool same-date harvests now appear as a single combined row.
Data integrity improvements and startup reliability fixes.
Pool and vault action locking and Add Capital vault mode simplified.
Update check, backup reliability, and various fixes.
Hedge tab — perpetual short sizing with IV and funding rate.
GEM alerts — Pushover integration for price targets.
History tab improvements and CSV export.
Pool alert system — Act Now and Watch tier notifications.
Vault close and partial withdrawal flow.
Holdings alerts — buy trigger, stop loss, T1/T2/T3.
Beat HODL? scorecard and annualized return calculation.
Yield log and harvest tracking.
Initial release — LP pools, vaults, holdings, GEMs.
